title: Knema retusa
taxonomy: Knema retusa is classified within the family Myristicaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the nutmeg family. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its relationship to other species within the family. The genus Knema is a group of species that are native to Southeast Asia and are known for their distinctive leaves and seeds. Myristicaceae is a family of around 180 species, many of which are used as food or have medicinal properties. Knema retusa's classification as a member of this family highlights its importance in the plant kingdom.

The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which could aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. intro: Knema retusa, also known as the 'Riddalo tree', is a species of evergreen tree in the family Myristicaceae. The health benefits of Knema retusa include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions. Bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Knema retusa include extracts and essential oils, often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
